summary: First iteration testing of Simran's PetVilla. Backend: 20/20 pytest cases passed against public URL covering services list/detail (6 slugs verified), reviews, blog list/detail (content excluded from list, included in detail), booking CRUD (create + GET persistence), contact creation, admin login (correct + wrong password 401), admin bookings list (401 without auth, list with token), PATCH status (valid transitions + invalid status 400 + 404 on missing booking + filter by status), admin contacts and admin stats. Frontend: homepage renders hero + 6 service cards + WhatsApp float (wa.me/919876543210 placeholder), service detail (/services/pet-boarding) renders, reviews/blog/blog-detail/about pages render, contact form successfully submits, admin login at /admin works with petvilla2026 and navigates to /admin/dashboard which renders stats (3/2/1/0/2), filter chips (All/New/Confirmed/Completed/Cancelled), bookings table with status badges, Bookings & Contact Leads tabs, Export CSV button, and View action. Booking wizard step 1 confirmed visually (Pet Boarding selectable, Continue button). Note: wizard is 5 steps (Service, Pet, Dates, Owner, Review) — PRD said 6 steps; this is a deviation worth confirming.
action_items: Confirm whether 5-step booking wizard (Service/Pet/Dates/Owner/Review) is acceptable vs PRD's stated 6-step flow. Current implementation is 5 steps — likely fine but flag to user.
WhatsApp number is the placeholder 919876543210; replace with real number before launch.
Razorpay & Brevo intentionally deferred per PRD — no action.
Consider adding data-testid attributes to booking wizard fields and admin elements to make automated end-to-end testing easier (current selectors rely on placeholder/order).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 540-line file mixing models + static catalog + routes; consider splitting (models.py, data/services.py, routers/admin.py) when it grows further.
Admin auth uses a hardcoded fallback ADMIN_TOKEN inside server.py (line 22). Token lives in .env which is correct, but the fallback default should be removed for production safety.
Booking endpoint does not validate that submitted service slugs exist in the SERVICES catalog — invalid slugs would silently be stored.
No rate limiting on POST /api/contact and POST /api/bookings — vulnerable to spam without WAF in front.
Static catalog (services/reviews/blogs) is hardcoded in Python; fine for Phase 1 but blogs/reviews should move to DB when admin needs to edit them.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% — homepage, services, reviews, blog, about, contact submit, admin login + dashboard all verified visually and functionally. Full 5-step booking wizard end-to-end UI walk-through could not be completed via Playwright due to selector library limitations, but step 1 verified and underlying POST /api/bookings is verified by backend tests (test bookings appear correctly in admin dashboard).

test_credentials: Admin password: petvilla2026 (also written nowhere in /app/memory/test_credentials.md — recommend main agent add it).
seed_data_creation: Created 3 TEST_ prefixed bookings + 1 TEST_ contact lead via API tests. They appear in admin dashboard. Cleanup not done — main agent may want to clear before user demo via Mongo or by adding a cleanup endpoint.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_petvilla_api.py covers all 12 documented endpoints with status + data assertions. Use BASE_URL from frontend/.env (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Admin token logic: POST /api/admin/login returns {token: ADMIN_TOKEN}, then send Authorization: Bearer <token>. WhatsApp number is a placeholder. Booking wizard is 5 steps not 6.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Wizard step-count discrepancy (5 vs 6) is a spec interpretation choice, not a bug — main agent to confirm with user.
